examples: The star is about 10,000 light-years from Earth, in the constellation Centaurus. [Photo of the massive star.]
Take Centaurus Advisors, a $5 billion energy hedge fund run by John Arnold, once a natural-gas trader at Enron.
The coldest known place in the universe is the Boomerang Nebula, 5, 000 light years away from us in the constellation Centaurus.
Centaurus A is the first galaxy to be studied with the Hubble's infrared camera.
Rather than trading natural gas, Arnold has spent 2012 winding down his fund, Centaurus Advisors.
